title: Phaulanthus griffithii
taxonomy: Phaulanthus griffithii is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and relationships to other species within this group. The genus Phaulanthus is also a part of this family, with Phaulanthus griffithii being one of its members. The classification of Phaulanthus griffithii is further refined within the Rutaceae family, where it is part of the tribe Rutoideae. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Phaulanthus griffithii within the Rutaceae family.

intro: Phaulanthus griffithii, commonly known as the Griffith's spurge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Phaulanthus griffithii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or ointment, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin infections and wounds.
